stories.title: Chat Control 1.0 and 2.0 Explained
stories.summary: A deep dive into the EU's Chat Control regulation proposals, which would mandate client-side scanning of encrypted messages — raising major privacy and AI surveillance concerns.

stories.title: Show HN: Davit — An Apple Containers UI
stories.summary: Davit is a new GUI for Apple's containerization framework, simplifying container management on macOS with a native interface.

stories.title: Beijing Looks at Curbing Overseas Access to China's Top AI Models
stories.summary: Chinese authorities have held meetings with top tech firms about potentially restricting overseas access to China's most advanced AI models, including unreleased ones, in a move that could reshape the global AI landscape.

stories.title: We're Extending Access to Fable 5 on All Paid Plans Through July 12
stories.summary: Anthropic is extending access to its Fable 5 frontier model across all paid Claude plans through July 12, making advanced capabilities more widely available.

stories.title: Show HN: Rowboat — Open-Source, Local-First Alternative to Claude Desktop
stories.summary: Rowboat is a new open-source, local-first desktop application that serves as an alternative to Claude Desktop, emphasizing privacy and offline capabilities.

stories.title: Meta Launches Muse Image AI Generator, Users Push Back Over Use of Their Photos
stories.summary: Meta has rolled out Muse Image, a new AI image generator, but users are already raising concerns about the platform using their photos for training data without consent.

stories.title: Why the Rise of Open Source AI Isn't Hurting Anthropic... Yet
stories.summary: Analysis of how Anthropic's enterprise focus and frontier model positioning have so far shielded it from open-source AI competition, though the landscape is shifting rapidly.

stories.title: Microsoft Joins AI Cost-Cutting Trend by Relying More on Its Own Models
stories.summary: Microsoft is shifting to rely more heavily on its own AI models to reduce costs, joining a broader industry trend of companies seeking to cut expensive third-party AI dependencies.

stories.title: Samsung Estimates 19-Fold Rise in Q2 Operating Profit, But Shares Slide on AI Jitters
stories.summary: Samsung reported a massive 19-fold jump in Q2 operating profit driven by AI chip demand, but shares fell as investors worry the AI boom may be losing momentum.

stories.title: Discord Admits AI Moderation Bug Wrongfully Banned Users Over Harmless Images
stories.summary: Discord has acknowledged a bug in its AI-powered moderation system that incorrectly flagged harmless images and banned users, raising questions about automated content moderation reliability.

stories.title: The First American Autonomous Ground Vehicles Are Fighting in Ukraine
stories.summary: Forterra's autonomous Lancer vehicles have become the first American-made autonomous ground combat vehicles deployed in active warfare in Ukraine.

stories.title: Insilico Medicine Advances AI Drug for IPF to Phase III Trials
stories.summary: Insilico Medicine has advanced its AI-discovered drug for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis to Phase III human clinical trials, marking a milestone for AI-driven pharmaceutical development.
